summary: End-to-end testing of EBA Finance Tracker (backend + Expo web). Backend pytest suite (20/20 passing) validated health, register/login/me, CRUD for income/expenses/startup/investments with user scoping and enum validation, /api/summary with correct multi-currency totals + available_cash math, monthly & category reports, and CSV export for all 5 kinds. Frontend flow tested end-to-end on mobile viewport: registration → dashboard shows 6 summary cards + EUR/TRY/GBP rows, added income (Acme Ltd €1,500), expense (Adobe €50), startup cost (Company Registration Notary €800), investment (€5,000), all reflected on dashboard (Total Income €1,500, Operating Expenses €50, Startup Costs €800, Personal Investment €5,000, Net Cash Balance €650, Available Cash €6,450 — math correct). Reports tabs Monthly/Category/Cash Flow render. Dark-mode toggle switches theme. ONE bug found: after tapping Sign Out from Settings, the app remains on /settings (no redirect to /(auth)/sign-in). Auth state clears (user name disappears) but there is no guard/redirect, leaving the tab shell visible.

ui_bugs.issue: Sign Out clears auth state but does not navigate away. User remains on /settings with tab bar visible and no way to reach sign-in without app reload. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/settings.tsx:92 (onPress={signOut}) — needs router.replace('/(auth)/sign-in') after signOut, or a global auth guard in (tabs)/_layout.tsx that redirects when user===null.

issues: Console warns: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — some component still uses shadowOffset/shadowOpacity etc. React Native Web recommends boxShadow. Non-blocking.

action_items: Add navigation on sign-out: after `await signOut()` (in settings.tsx onPress) call `router.replace('/(auth)/sign-in')`, OR add an auth guard in app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx that redirects to sign-in when user===null (recommended — protects every tab, not just settings).
Optionally replace legacy shadow* style props with boxShadow for cross-platform correctness (silences RN Web deprecation warnings).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py already excludes _id via projection {"_id": 0} — good.
Auth token stored via expo-secure-store (via src/utils/storage) — good.
All backend routes properly prefixed with /api — good.
server.py uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI 0.109+; consider migrating to lifespan handler.
server.py is 500 lines — still within limits, but could split routers by resource if it grows.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all screens + all CRUD flows work; sign-out redirect missing)

seed_data_creation: Two fresh test users registered via API (TEST_a_*, TEST_b_*) — data cleaned up via teardown fixtures. One test user registered via UI (ui2_*@example.com) with income/expense/startup/investment records; not cleaned up (small, isolated).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid — 20/20 pytest passes. If retesting only frontend sign-out fix: (1) register a new user via UI, (2) navigate to Settings tab, (3) tap [data-testid=settings-signout], (4) assert page.url ends with /sign-in AND [data-testid=sign-in-email-input] is visible within 3s. Tab bar text-selectors matched via aria-label suffix (see click_tab helper) since Expo Router adds icon glyph before label.
rca_of_issue: Sign-out bug RCA: In /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/settings.tsx the Pressable at line 91-92 calls signOut directly (`onPress={signOut}`). signOut (src/ctx/auth.tsx L48-51) only clears secure store token and setUser(null). No navigation is triggered. Also, app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx has no auth-guard useEffect. app/index.tsx does route based on user but is only mounted at '/' — after signOut the route is still '/settings', so index.tsx effect never runs. Fix options: (A) simplest — wrap the settings signout handler: `onPress={async () => { await signOut(); router.replace('/(auth)/sign-in'); }}`. (B) better — in app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx add `useEffect(() => { if (!loading && !user) router.replace('/(auth)/sign-in'); }, [user, loading])` so the guard also covers deep-linked unauthenticated access.
